entry.title: Unlock your green supply chain with SAP Sustainability Data Exchange
entry.content:
📢#sapsustainability #supplychain #sapcloud #s4hana #clouderp #sustainability #risewithsap

Join our live virtual session to learn how SAP Sustainability Data Exchange can help you streamline and simplify the process of collecting and sharing Scope 3 emissions data with your business partners based on industry standards. Discover how this innovative solution empowers your procurement and supply chain management to support your sustainability strategy and pave the way for a greener future. Don't miss this opportunity to learn about the future of sustainable business practices!

Deep Dive: We will be offering a deep dive session on February 19.
Make sure you register to attend live or get a recording:
https://sap-se.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_AcJ5ulneTPazNh1nnutwzw

🎤Speakers:
Tim van Kerkhof - Product Manager of SAP Sustainability Data Exchange
Leyi Liu - Product Marketing Manager of SAP Sustainability Data Exchange

➡️Host: Larissa Brinkmann - Global SAP User Groups Organization

entry.title: Drill down Story reports successfactors
entry.content:
Good day!

I am currently setting up Story reports in successfactors.

I am trying to find the Explorer Available (drill down) option in the report widgets, however I cannot identify them.

Can you please tell me how to add this feature to the widgets?

Thanks

Regards

entry.title: Extract Signal Queries into Document or PDF in SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Hello There,

Good Day!!

Is there any way to extract all the Signal queries(all widgets Signals)  into a Word Document or Excel. Just like in Process Manager we do as Reporting->Process documentation (word) or Process documentation (PDF).

Thanks in Advance.

Shravan R.

entry.title: Re: 🟣 Set Up Rules for Who Can Create and Transport SAP Build Projects
entry.content:
All projects created in lobby /transport from lobby will be in scope.

CALM integration is not in place yet for SAP Build

entry.title: Re: Business Process Integration certificate
entry.content: Hi, Did you finish the certification exam? I'm about to take in the upcoming months so could you please guide me about any resources, your experience, or any tips and insights?

entry.title: Re: Will there be any impact if we change the field selec...
entry.content:
As long as you only using NBB for NB--and making the requisitioner field mandatory for NBB --you should be okay-- your ZNB will have a new field key ZNBB and in that field, you will not have that issue---But you run few scenarios applicable to your system --e.g. 

All NB PRs--old or new this field is mandatory now-- so any time you go and change old PR that do not have this value --you need to supply it -- so consider updating old PRs as well 

PRs generated from MRP contain MRP controller names --if you are expecting it should be userid --consider making a change in the config for MRP controller as well to reflect the same.

Your PRs are created from ME51N or you have PRs created from order or network or other source you need to make sure you supply this field or make it mandatory on that end too.

Good luck !!

entry.title: Re: Suppress Custom uibb in sap mdg Customer
entry.content:
Hi Karthik,

Please try setting the UIBB property - HIDDEN ELEMENT to "Hidden and excluded from event loop". 

Regards,
Saravanan

entry.title: Accelerate your period-end journal entries with AI-assisted Journal Upload Cases
entry.content:
With the S/4Hana Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS3 delivery, SAP would deliver a feature that accelerates period-end journal entries with AI aid. Period-end journal entries are necessary for a period-closing activity. Finance teams spend considerable time curating data according to policies and reporting needs. SAP Business AI for Finance offers AI-assisted Journal Upload Cases that streamline operations, automate processes, and generate period-end entries aligned with posting policies faster than ever. This empowers better business decisions and transforms your team into a lean strategic finance function.  This solution is ready to use for your specific business needs without the need for IT support. (Use of this feature may require some additional commercial agreements, please get in touch with your SAP account executive for further details.) 

This blog post explains the steps to using AI-assisted Journal Upload Cases. One example of recurring period-end tasks is creating accrual postings for shuttle bus expenses incurred by different departments. This is one example of recurring tasks that can be seamlessly performed utilizing AI-assisted Journal Upload Cases.

1. Prerequisites

AI-Assisted Journal Upload Cases require AI to be scoped in an S/4Hana Cloud Private Edition system. SAP has delivered an ISLM (Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management) scenario, FINS_ACDOC_AI_MJUC, which needs to be deployed and activated in a customer system.

Before activating the above scenario, you need to get AI service keys. Please follow the required SAP ticket process to get the necessary keys by creating a ticket on component CA-S4H-BTPAI. Once you have the required data (OAuth 2.0 Client Profile & Service Key (JSON format)) available, you need to update the same in the system using the below steps.

Update the service key details in the ISLM scenario via the below IMG tree:

ABAP Platform > Application Server > Basis Services > Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management > Service Connections for Machine Learning Infrastructure > Maintain Intelligent Scenario Connections

After updating the AI key information, the ISLM scenario must be deployed and activated in the system. This can be done using the Fiori app (App ID F4470): Intelligent Scenario Management.

 

 

1.1 Authorizations

The Fiori apps for posting policy and journal cases are added to the below artifacts:

Business Catalog: SAP_SFIN_BC_GL_JE_PROC (General Ledger—Journal Entry Processing)

Technical Catalog: SAP_TC_FIN_ACC_COMMON (SAP Financials - Accounting: Fiori Apps)

Business Group: SAP_SFIN_BCG_DOC_ENTRY (Journal Entries)

Business Role: SAP_BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT (General Ledger Accountant)

1.2 Number Ranges

Number range object: IJU_CASE

Use T-Code SNRO and maintain a number range interval for the journal case object.

2. Configuration

Two configuration activities, the Journal Case Category Code and Case Subcategory Code, enable customers to categorize their different use cases for manual journal uploads. For example, suppose you have two other policies for two instances of accruals. In that case, you will create one category code and two subcategory codes inside that these policies can be uploaded into the system and later used by journal cases. 

2.1 Journal Upload Case Category Codes

It is accessible in the following IMG tree: Financial Accounting > Financial Accounting Global Settings > Document > Intelligent Journal Uploads > Define Journal Upload Case Categories

 

2.2 Journal Upload Case Subcategory Codes

It is accessible in the following IMG tree: Financial Accounting > Financial Accounting Global Settings > Document > Intelligent Journal Uploads > Define Journal Upload Case Subcategories

 

3. Posting Policies

You can upload your posting policies in the system with the above settings. In 2023 FPS1, we are only supporting Excel format. To get a template for a posting policy, please get in touch with SAP refer to note: 3561821

A posting policy is a list of instructions for various fields on a standard journal entry header and item levels. A sample file screenshot is as follows:

The Fiori app below creates a posting policy in the system and uploads the document.

Fiori app: Manual Journal Posting Policies (App ID: F8539)

Business Role to access the app: SAP_BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT

The app would be accessible via General Ledger > Journal Entries

Select appropriate category and subcategory codes, describe your policy, and provide a “Valid From” date. Once the policy is active, it will be valid per the provided “Valid From” date. The “Valid To” date is defaulted to 31.12.9999. 

The system validates if the provided “Valid From” date is possible based on the existing active policies for that category and subcategory codes. The system will give an error if an active policy with a “Valid From” date after the provided date exists. The system will allow a “Valid From” date if no active policy with “Valid From” exists on or after that date.

Let’s understand from the illustration below.

If you try to create a new policy with the “Valid From” date 01.07.2024, the system will not allow it as another policy is valid from 15.07. However, the system will allow you to create a new policy valid from 16.07.2024.

In 2023 FPS3, the system cannot detect gaps in datelines for a policy specific to a category and subcategory code. Thus, as the “Valid From” date is a user-entry field, there might be gaps if the user does not ensure proper dates to avoid gaps.

After uploading the policy document, you can proceed with activation. The button Active in the screenshot below activates the document.

Activation is a background process that may take around 4-5 minutes. During activation, all the instructions are extracted from the uploaded policy document and sent to an AI engine to map with the system's existing capabilities. The policy is not activated if the AI engine cannot map one or more instructions. The errors are visible in the Application Log on the Fiori app. In 2023 FPS3, the Fiori app does not have a view to show the mapped capabilities. This is something that is being worked on for the next release cycle.

The supported capabilities are as follows:

  1. SplitConditon: Splitting of journal entries based on specific attributes. For example, journal entries should be split for company codes.
  2. Aggregation: Aggregation of amounts based on specific attributes. For example, the aggregate document currency amount for each cost center and vendor ID.
  3. Copy: Copy the value from an Excel field. For example, the Company ID (BUKRS) value is taken from the Company column in the transaction Excel.
  4. Assign: Assign a fixed value to an attribute. For example, the G/L account has a fixed value defined in the transaction Excel.
  5. Evaluate Condition: Evaluate a condition and then calculate the value for an attribute. For example, Post Key is 40 for positive and 50 for negative amounts.
  6. MaxLines: Set the maximum number of line items in a journal entry. For example, the posting policy may have an instruction stating that a journal entry cannot have more than five line items.
  7. Concatenate: Concatenates different parameters as specified in a posting policy.

Once the policy is active, you can proceed with case creation.

4. Journal Cases

To create a journal case, you should be ready with the data that you want to upload to that case. This data relates to the transactions you prefer to be considered for journal entry creation. There is no standard format for preparing your data except that it should be available as an Excel file.

The first row should be the header row, with the names of the fields corresponding to the data's semantics.

System Validations on data file:

  1. An error will be raised if the column names mentioned in the posting policy file are not present in Excel.
  2. If mandatory fields required to create a journal entry in the system are missing in Excel, an error will be raised.

A sample data file is illustrated below:

 

4.1 Create Journal Case

The Fiori app below creates a journal case in the system and uploads the document.

Fiori app: Manual Journal Upload Cases (App ID: F8538)

Business Role to access the app: SAP_BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT

The app would be accessible via General Ledger > Journal Entries

Describe your case, case category, subcategory codes, and your fiscal period with year and variants while you create a new case. A relevant policy is fetched based on the entered category, subcategory codes, and the last date of the fiscal period. If a policy is found, it is linked under the Guidance section.

You can then upload the Excel file containing transactions. The system will validate the data, either issue an error or accept the file. If the file is accepted, the status changes from Created to Transaction Data Uploaded.

4.2 Generate Proposal

Once the transaction data is uploaded, you can trigger journal entry proposal generation by clicking the Generate Proposal button. Proposal generation is a background process and may take some time. Once the process is over, the details will be available in the Application Log.

If the proposal generation is successful, the status changes to Proposal Generated. If errors occur during the generation process, the status changes to Proposal Generation Failed.

Error handling: If there are errors, you need to go to the Application Log to check them.

  1. If the errors are related to uploaded data, you must fix them in your Excel file. When you have fixed your data in Excel, open the relevant Journal Case and click Edit. Delete the uploaded file from the Source of Transaction Data section, and the status changes to Created. Upload the new file; the status changes to Transaction Data Uploaded. Click the Generate Proposal button again; the status changes to Proposal Generation In Process.

If the errors are related to a system configuration, you must fix them with those settings. After fixing the settings, you can trigger proposal generation again by clicking the Generate Proposal button.

4.3 Validate Proposal

After the successful proposal generation, you can proceed with proposal validation. Click the Validate Proposal button to start the validation process; the status changes to Validation In Process. Validation is a background process and may take some time. Once the process is over, the status is changed accordingly.

In case the validation is successful, the status changes to Proposal Validated. If the validation process runs into errors, the status changes to Validation Failed.

Error handling: If there are errors, you need to go to the Application Log to check them.

  1. If the errors are related to uploaded data, you must fix them in your Excel file. When you have fixed your data in Excel, open the relevant Journal Case and click on the Discard Proposal button. The status changes to Transaction Data Uploaded. Click on Edit and delete the uploaded file from the Source of Transaction Data section, and the status changes to Created. Upload the new file; the status changes to Transaction Data Uploaded. Click the Generate Proposal button again; the status changes to Proposal Generation In Process.
  2. If the errors are related to a system configuration, you must fix them with those settings. After fixing the settings, you can trigger proposal validation again by clicking the Validate Proposal button.

4.4 Posting Proposal

After the successful validation, the user can proceed with the posting by clicking the Post button. This will trigger a background process and may take some time to complete.

On successful postings, the status changes to Complete. Corresponding journal entries (JEs) are created in the relevant general ledgers. The created JE IDs are updated in the proposal line items of the journal case. 

If there is an error, the status changes to Posting Failed. The user can check the errors in the Application Log.

Error handling: If there are errors, you need to go to the Application Log to check them.

  1. If the errors are related to uploaded data, you must fix them in your Excel file. When you have fixed your data in Excel, open the relevant Journal Case and click on the Discard Proposal button. The status changes to Transaction Data Uploaded. Click on Edit and delete the uploaded file from the Source of Transaction Data section, and the status changes to Created. Upload the new file; the status changes to Transaction Data Uploaded. Click the Generate Proposal button again; the status changes to Proposal Generation In Process.

If the errors are related to a system configuration, you must fix them with those settings. After fixing the settings, you can trigger posting again by clicking the Post button.

entry.title: Re: Asset Management, Corrective Maintenance Order Type
entry.content:
Hi,

That's an older scope item.

  • 'BH1 Corrective Maintenance' was superseded with '4HH Reactive Maintenance'
  • 'BJ2 Preventive Maintenance' was superseded with '4HI Proactive Maintenance'

I would recommend referring to and using the '4HH Reactive Maintenance' test script instead of the one you are currently using.

You likely don't have the older scope item activated in your system (which is why the YBA1 order type is not available to choose)

Tim

entry.title: Re: How to Validate the Data for Field in CDS View

entry.title: How to deactivate cost center for cost element
entry.content: If a G/L account is a cost element, when I enter a posting with this account, the system will require me to enter a cost object, such as cost center. However, for some accounts like city tax, I don't want to allocate it to any cost center for cost analysis. So how can I deactivate this validation when I use this cost element account in SAP cloud?

entry.title: Re: Report on who is logged in and what client level - IBP

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Excel Add-in Table Default Number...
entry.content:
Hi @DBalinski ,

You can change data type decimal places by default in model. 

STEP 1. You have to click on measure and select appropriate decimal places.

In the measure you have to change data type as per your requirement here as shown in pic 7 you can change as per your requirement by default in model

Here you can change just change it will default set 

Kindly upvote if you find it useful.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: IRPA/BPA working with tables within S/4 Web/SAPUI5
entry.content: Hello, I just saw that you have multiple post related to screen scraping/desktop automation for BPA. Just wanted to check, prior concluding the application automation have you checked if there is an available API that can be used for getting/posting the parameters using BPA?

entry.title: GRC Tuesdays: What's New in SAP solutions for International Trade Management, Enhancing Compliance
entry.content:
“New trade sanctions become operational” is a headline that one can regularly read these days.

For many companies – especially in the financial sector or sensitive industries, this has been a reality for a long time. But more and more organizations are realizing that these compliance requirements apply to them because they have dealings in geographies or with stakeholders that are now in the scope of a sanction regime.

Let’s roll back and start with the basics:

 

What are sanctions?

 

In short, sanctions are political and economic decisions resulting in restrictions placed on trade and financial activities. Failure to comply creates an offence that might lead to penalty measures.

And the list of activities that constitutes an offense can be quite long.

For instance, “trading, importing, exporting, selling, purchasing, transferring, transiting or transporting goods” of course, but also “providing brokering services, technical assistance or other services”, “making funds or economic resources available directly or indirectly to, or for the benefit of, a designated person, entity or body”, “failing to freeze funds or economic resources belonging to or owned, held or controlled by a designated person, entity or body”, “providing financial services or performing financial activities”… and more are detailed in the 2024 update to the EU Directive on the definition of criminal offences and penalties for the violation of Union restrictive measures.  

There are currently 14 ongoing sanctions regimes enforced by the UN Security Council, but many states – or economic unions, have their own set of sanction measures addressing specific sectors, territories or individuals. This is the case for the United States, the European Union, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, China, Canada, or Australia just to name a few.

Enforcing these ever-evolving measures is by no means an easy task, and keeping track of all the changes adds to the complexity. But breaking these sanctions is definitely not an option as, at the very least, it will lead to bad reputation, but also fines, loss of license to operate or even criminal charges can be enforced.

And, with predefined easy to use content from Mendel Verlag, this is precisely what SAP Watch List Screening has been designed to support as I am sure you already know.

But there are also cases where companies want to screen against custom watch lists: either coming from a new content provider, or maybe even internal restrictions, potentially conflicting parties, etc.

 

Introducing Custom Watch List Uploading

 

With the November 2024 update, Product Management and Engineering teams for SAP solutions for International Trade Management have introduced a powerful new feature: the ability to upload custom watch list content. This new capability enables organizations to enhance their screening processes proactively, safeguarding the company against potential risks arising from being involved with dubious stakeholders.

You can now seamlessly integrate your own watch list content into our SAP Watch List Screening solution using an API.

This functionality allows users to:

Personalize Screening Processes

Tailor the screening to your organization’s specific needs by adding entities that are particularly relevant to your operations. For example, this includes lists of fraudulent taxpayers, or persons and organizations with whom you prefer not to do business

Stay Up-to-Date

Regularly update your custom watch lists to reflect the latest information pertinent to your industry or region

Leverage Additional Data Provider Content

Include lists from different providers, or additional lists provided by Mendel Verlag which are not included in the sanctioned party list screening packages offered

The upload and management of custom watch lists are entirely under your charge, giving you full control over the content you screen against.

To achieve this, a new user-friendly Configuration app has been released:

 With this new Fiori app, users can:

Configure Watch List Package Categories

 

Assign appropriate categories to the content provided by Mendel Verlag, ensuring accurate organization and screening

Manage Screening Settings

 

Decide whether your uploaded custom watch list should be active and used during the screening process, giving you full control over compliance operations

Monitor Watch List Fill Levels

 

Keep track of the volume and status of your uploaded custom watch lists to ensure they are comprehensive and effective

 

Getting Started

 

If you are already using SAP Watch List Screening and would like to leverage this new functionality, simply follow the steps below:

 

 

 

Prepare Your Custom Watch List Content

Gather the data you wish to include in your custom watch list. This could be entities you prefer to avoid, or additional lists from data providers

 

 

Use the API for Upload

Utilize SAP’s API to upload your custom watch list content into the SAP Watch List Screening system

 

 

Configure with the New App

Access the new app to set up your watch list package categories, determine screening usage, and monitor fill levels

 

Find out more information

 

Should you be interested in reading more, you will find additional details at the following pages:

  • Feature in What's New in SAP Watch List Screening
  • Dedicated section in the Administration Guide for SAP Watch List Screening
  • REST API description in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Credits for the information in this blog go to Valentin Kruck and David Sommer, leading SAP Watch List Screening for Product Management and Engineering at SAP.

And if you are interested in learning more about SAP solutions for Governance, Risk, and Compliance, feel free to fill-in the demo request form!

entry.title: Re: Request for Adjustment of Movement Type for Goods Rec...
entry.content:
I do not think you can create inbound delivery using VL31N or VL10B without PO--However -- you may need to create new inbound delivery like ZEL--Then using Item category ELP which already configured 501 movement type -- but you have use VL01NO and supply Sales org data -shipping point etc and setup some master data or more additional config--not sure - i have not done it myself but you can try if this work out for you.

You also have to allow Ship to Party for Partner determination procedure EL in order to enter ship to party--which can equal to plant code --

entry.title: Freight Cost Allocation and Service Entry Sheet not generated while posting the Freight Accrual.
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

I am using the S4/HANA Public Cloud TM solution for our client with 6w1, 6W2, 6W3, and 6W4 activated.

Based on my understanding, after the accrual posting by using the app Monitor Accrual Postings, the system is expected to generate the following documents:

Freight Cost Allocation
Purchase Order (PO)
Service Entry Sheet
I am able to create the Purchase Order → Freight Unit → Freight Order → Delivery successfully. However, when I try to post the document in the Monitor Accrual Postings app, the system posts the document but no Freight Cost calculation document is generated.

Additionally, upon checking the Manage Purchase Order app, I can see that the PO is being created in the background, but no Service Entry Sheet is being generated.

Could you please assist in resolving this issue?

entry.title: Re: Does SAP Mobile Start support Zebra laser barcode sca...
entry.content:
Hello,

Normally, you can catch the scan from in your ui5 app in any browser. I don’t know if it will work in SAP Start but it should be possible to do this using google chrome. You are not forced to use the zebra browser. Here you have some possibilities: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/21633537/javascript-how-to-read-a-hand-held-barcode-scanner-best

entry.title: Companion get context id of tab page
entry.content:
We are trying to show some simulations using the ID screen context, this works for specific apps, we can pick the ID from the URL. So adding this to the sim context works "#Material-createInMDG".

But we want to do the same for some screens that have multiple apps inside a tab, when looking at the URL we see this "#Launchpad-openFLPPage?pageId=ZS4_TRE_ASSETPROC_P" but adding this as a screen ID does not seem to work.

Is there another way to get the ID of these pages if possible?

entry.title: Re: SAP ByD connectivity with SAP IS and SAP DS
entry.content:
Hi vikas_goswami,

Could you please provide more details on this query? Are you trying to use the app Migrate Your Data app in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition? Typically we provide XML/CSV template file for each of pre-defined migration object.
May I know what do you mean by "profile and write rules on ByD data in Information Steward"?

Best regards,
Irene

entry.title: Re: How to deactivate cost center for cost element

Added Cloud Finance tag. Please remove other tags.

Q&A for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - How to ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Migration from SAP BW 7.5 to SAP BW/4HAN...
entry.content:
Hello Amir,

A conversion to BW/4 needs to be prepared very well.

To 1:

One important step is to decide how to "slice and dice" your BW system to create scopes (you call it "module") to convert the objects into BW/4 compatible objects. This means it has to be analyzed how objects can be grouped to convert as a package. You start with start objects which collect dependent other objects, e. g. a CUBE will collect the MPROs on top of it, because the MPRO can not contain ADSO. When selecting start objects you have to keep in mind that there are BW objects which are collectors on its own, e. g. Dataflow object or Process Chain. If you use them as a start object, the toolbox will collect all dependent objects, e. g. a process chain contains of ODSO activate, so the ODSO will be collected to, and the transformations into the ODSO and the DTP into the ODSO, etc. So be careful with the selection of the start objects.

To 2:

The ECC system can remain as source system for BW/4, but it has to be switched to an ODP source system type. DataSource have to be made ODP enabled and the toolbox will support the "switch" from SAPI extraction to the ODP framework. Delta initializations will remain ("Delta queue cloning"); no new initialization is required. After the conversion of the datasource the objects are transferred: InfoPackage -> DTP and delta loading can be continued (with new objects) but within the ODP framework.

The conversion guide and SAP Note 2383530 gives you more information.

Best Regards,

Eduard

entry.title: Re: Will there be any impact if we change the field selec...
entry.content:
Hello @Fivestars 

Changing ZNB field selection won't affect already created ZNB PRs. Field selection setting drives a behavior of ME51N / ME52N / ME53N transactions, it doesn't update existing documents. However ZNB field selection will take effect if you try to change an existing ZNB PR in ME52N.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: How to deactivate cost center for cost element
entry.content: Hello, If the account is not cost object relevant, you should use other account type. Best Regards, Owen

entry.title: Re: #COE#BUMA Extensibility to add custom field in Maintenance order cost - Plan/Actual app.
entry.content:

Suggest you read below Blog for tips of getting your answers faster:

Q&A for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - How to ... - SAP Community

entry.title: SO-DR-SI upload through DTW
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

 

May I request an Excel or TXT file of SO-DR-SI for uploading through DTW? I cannot use the sample file in my database. Additionally, I need these documents to be properly linked after uploading them in DTW.

 

Thank you,

entry.title: Re: How to control pre-defined asset class
entry.content:
Added Finance tag. Please remove other tags.

Suggest you read below Blog for tips of getting your answers faster:

Q&A for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - How to ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: CRM Utilities - Authorization restriction in contract...
entry.content:
Hi Christian,

the IS-U Contracts in WebClient are based on the CRM OneOrder model for business transactions. Thus the same authorization concept applies on S/4 as it is on CRM.

I suppose you are already assigning Sales Organization data to the Contracts via an Org. Determination Profile. The correct PFCG object for your scenario should be CRM_ORD_OE.

Have a look here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CUSTOMER_RELATIONSHIP_MANAGEMENT/cb77bf16eae9401fb7c569c5ac3b4bb6/d0bee8532a84424de10000000a174cb4.html?&version=7.0.4.19

cheers Carsten

entry.title: Re: Convert DMS Folder ID to Name in Automation
entry.content: This is nice, thanks Dan.

entry.title: Can we customize the process template of workflow in cloud ready mode in sap MDG
entry.content:
Dear Team,

As we are aware, MDG on S/4HANA 2023 supports Cloud Ready Mode. Based on my understanding, in Cloud Ready Mode, rule-based workflows cannot be used, and instead, build process automation workflows must be implemented.

As per my client’s requirements, we need to customize the build process automation workflow. If anyone has encountered a similar requirement, please confirm whether customization is possible or share any relevant documentation that could help us achieve this.

Looking forward to your insights.

Best regards,
Rahul Khandelwal

@0012anirban @Mark63

entry.title: Re: From ECC to S4H Public Cloud - GOS Attachment Migration
entry.content:
Hi matt_malone，

Could you please add the tag ABAP Development and remove all other tags? Kindly check this Blog for more information on the tags.
Select correct tags will help on finding the correct person on unswering your questions.

Best regards,
Irene

entry.title: Re: How to Create, Enhance and Adapt Floorplan Manager Applications for SAP_UI 7.57
entry.content:
Hi Jens,

for SAP_UI 758 you can use this guide, too. There are no significant changes compared to SAP_UI 757.

Best regards,

Simon

entry.title: Re: From ECC to S4H Public Cloud - GOS Attachment Migration
entry.content:
Hi matt_malone，

I also found two blogs that you may be interested in.

SAP GOS Objects Migration -Download/Upload

SAP GOS Objects Migration - Upload Part 2

Hope this helps.

Kind regards,
Irene

entry.title: API that can get Cost Center and Manager fields by querying with worker's email
entry.content:
Hi all,

I would like to get the cost center and manager fields from "Manage Workforce" fioir app by querying with worker's email in S4hana public cloud.

So, which API can get these all data?

If one API cannot get these all data at once, could you please provide the APIs that can get these data by querying with worker's email. 

Best Regards, 

Aye Chan

no, these vendors are all the same format

entry.title: Re: Can we customize the process template of workflow in...
entry.content:
Dear Rahul,

Please find the official SAP Help Documentation for "Custom Workflow and Visibility": https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/6d52de87aa0d4fb6a90924720a5b0549/f75fe5de14a64d03b5fbf35f37dc8711.html 

Here is the link for the whole documentation around "Configuration of Workflow and Visibility" https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/6d52de87aa0d4fb6a90924720a5b0549/f547521d389d4d6286e8b8eff1f9e1d4.html 

Best regards,

Christina

entry.title: Recently Certified but do not know how to do my first step
entry.content:
Hello everyone!🙌🏼

I'm thrilled to announce that I've just earned my SAP Technology certification and I'm ready to embark on an exciting new adventure as an SAP consultant! I'm eager to get started, but I'm looking for advice on how to take my first steps in this field.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Using MAX Formula in SAP Build Apps - Only...
entry.content:
If you want the max of a certain, use a formula like this. I used Northwind's Product entity, and wanted the maximum unit price among all the products.

MAX(PLUCK(data.Employees1,"UnitPrice"))

 If you want the records, you can just sort the list:

SORT_BY_KEY(data.Employees1, "UnitPrice","desc")

The returned JSON is not like a database that has the ability to think of the data as rows and columns. It is just JSON, as far as I understand.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports Error Vendor Code 916
entry.content:
Hi RKRUTZFELDT,

As multiple company databases appear to be involved, please refer to SAP Note 2907995 for further information.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: Companion get context id of tab page
entry.content:
Hi Carlos,

When you enter editing mode in SAP Companion by clicking the pencil you will see a gear icon just above the pencil icon.  When you click the gear icon and select Show Context Information, you will see something like the following image:

The Screen ID value is the value being used by SAP Companion for context.  This is the context value used by the standard framework so if it doesn't include the ID value that you mention in your question, that value is not used.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: How to migrate batch specific UOM in Product master?
entry.content:
Hi

I searched in Help Portal for Migration object, and found two objects which may relevant to your query.

Batch unique at material and client level & Material Inventory Balance

The first one supports configure batches at material level and have fields like characteristics value, and the second one can be used to migrate base unit of measure or any alternative unit of measure defined in the material master data.

While in both of the documents, there is no statement mentioning to migrate batch specific UOM.

Migration objects internally calls BAPI to migrate data and follows the best practice of S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. If you can not find any proper migration object in Migrate Your Data app, then it is not supported yet. 

Please kindly use corresponding application on creating Product master data.

Also kindly post this valuable feedback on our Customer Influence Portal. Foe details kindly check 2676589 - How to Submit Feature Request for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Data Migration. These requests are reviewed by our product management and valid cases can be considered for the future product roadmap.

Best regards,
Irene

entry.title: Re: SAP SQL Anywhere RESTORE with RENAME - What is the correct script for restoring database
entry.content: Hello, Chris!

entry.title: OUT NOW: SAP Signavio February 2025 Release - Explore the Features to Help You Drive Transformation
entry.content:
The SAP Signavio February 2025 Release is here. Packed with new features and enhancements, this release helps you tackle key challenges such as prioritizing initiatives, simplifying workflows, and showcasing the value of your transformation efforts. Whether you’re a process owner, a process manager, or a transformation lead aiming to achieve measurable outcomes in your transformation projects, this release is designed with you in mind.

This release unveils highlights such as root cause analysis, value analysis capabilities, and enriched usability improvements. These updates ensure faster time to insights, improved transparency, and streamlined processes while reducing barriers to adoption.

Here's what’s in for you:

  • Advanced AI capabilities to simplify complex processes and deliver actionable insights.
  • Tools to calculate the monetary value of transformation initiatives, helping you prioritize and clearly communicate ROI.
  • Enhanced benchmarking analytics to compare your processes to industry standards and uncover opportunities for optimization.
  • Automated root cause analysis to identify performance drivers and resolve inefficiencies faster.
  • Expanded value analysis for process mining, exposing financial opportunities hidden in your data.
  • A redesigned process data pipeline-interface makes creating, integrating, and managing data workflows easier.
  • Flexible dashboard filtering for customized views that focus on what matters most to your role.
  • Improved workflows for creating connections, sourcing data, and building data with ease

Whether you’re leading a team, managing day-to-day operations, or setting strategic priorities, these features will make your work more efficient and your outcomes more impactful.

Ready to take your transformation to the next level? Read on to find out what this release brings for each SAP Signavio Transformation Suite component!

process AI Capabilities

Prompt your BPMN diagrams with the new text to process feature

Process modeling for an enterprise can cost hundreds of thousands of dollars annually, factoring in the time it takes to collect inputs and create or update hundreds of process models. By leveraging the AI-assisted process modeler capability, this amount can be reduced significantly while giving time back to be then used to customers’ advantage.

The new text to process feature streamlines process modeling, allowing modelers to convert textual descriptions into BPMN diagrams effortlessly. This feature enables users to minimize manual effort and accelerate the creation of process models, freeing up more time for optimization and analysis. This feature is planned for March 2025.

Process AI Capabilities - text to process

Analyze processes with the new text to widget feature

In November, the AI-assisted process analyzer, text to insights feature (in beta) was released.  Now, as part of the same beta program, we are releasing a new feature of the AI-assisted process analyzer, namely the so-called text to widget.

With the text to widget feature of the process analyzer, users can describe their queries using natural language and immediately generate new dashboard widgets (such as value charts and bar charts) that they can add to their workspace for continuous monitoring. All users can visualize data in the most relevant and intuitive way, and regardless of their technical expertise, they can create their own analyses independently. Visual representations make fact-based decisions easier, clearer, and faster, helping teams communicate findings, align efforts, and collaborate effectively with other stakeholders.

Process AI Capabilities - AI-assisted process analyzer, text to widget

Discover the Joule integration with SAP Signavio solutions

Another capability announced in November next to the aforementioned 'text to widget' is the integration of the SAP AI co-pilot Joule into SAP Signavio and its availability to select SAP Signavio customers in Germany and the US as part of an Early Adopter Care Program. With the February release, new   use cases have been added, meaning that users can now accomplish even more daily tasks in Joule. As an example, we have expanded the 'informational' use cases that Joule can support. The co-pilot can answer questions (e.g., "Joule, tell me how...") by drawing from the help documentation of various SAP Signavio products, minimizing search times for users. In parallel, new navigational (e.g., "tell me where") and transactional use cases (e.g., "do this task") have also been introduced.

Registrations to the Joule Early Adopter Care Program are open for a few more days until the end of February 

process AI capabilities – Joule Integration

Process Analysis and Mining

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

Articulate value with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

Value Analysis for Process Mining is designed to calculate and measure potential value discovered during data-driven investigations.

This powerful capability leverages embedded and standardized metrics, allowing for out-of-the-box adoption upon release. Additionally, these metrics can be customized to meet specific needs.

Key Highlights:

  • Comprehensive Value Calculations: Provided per single value driver and summarized for both one-off and recurring costs/benefits.
  • Evolution of Value Dashboards: If you're familiar with our existing "value dashboards" from the value accelerator library, this new capability is an evolution of those tools, offering enhanced functionality and insights.

Now, the entire value calculation capability is built into our product, scalable, reusable, and seamlessly integrated within the larger suite context.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – Value Analysis

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature.

Enhance Process Performance with Automated Root Cause Analysis

Automated Root Cause Analysis enhances process performance by delivering instant insights into key drivers for the Supplier Payment from Purchasing process. Testing every plausible hypothesis ensures no improvement opportunity is missed, allowing process analysts to focus on operational excellence.

This new feature of SAP Signavio Process Intelligence will allow you to democratize continuous process improvement by performing said analysis and vastly improving the understanding of underlying causes and potential mitigation actions.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – Automated Root Cause Analysis

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature

Be more efficient with improved UX and UI for data pipelines

The user flow for creating new process data pipelines has been renewed to enhance the user experience. The new UI design in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence aims to guide the user step-by-step while creating a process data pipeline. It helps the user add new data connections, define how to extract data from source systems and generate event logs that are ready for process analysis more easily. Users are empowered to adopt templates to accelerate the creation of process data pipelines by reusing ready-to-use content and available cross-source systems.

The enhanced interface lowers users' entry barriers with its clean and optimized user experience. 

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – improved UI and UX for data pipelines

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature.

Focus on relevant data with page-level filtering in dashboards

A key dashboard enhancement allows pages to display different data and perspectives with filtering options. This gives analysts greater flexibility to focus on relevant data subsets, improving usability efficiency. Each dashboard page can contain different data and perspectives that the user caters to.

This capability allows analysts to tailor the data selected for each individual page of the dashboard, improving usability and flexibility in analysis. It also allows the user to focus more effectively on the most relevant subset of data that elicits a behavior.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – Page level filtering in dashboards

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature.

Keep track of your changes with the new version history capability

Enhance the user experience for data modelers, particularly with the new Version History Capability, allowing for effective restoration to previously saved versions. Seamless integration enables code comparisons with the existing code, thus providing clear insight into the changes made over time.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – Version History CapabilityClick here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature.

SAP Signavio Process Insights

Put a value tag to improvements across different company segments

SAP Signavio Process Insights has enhanced value analysis for this release, allowing customers to determine the potential value of process improvements for specific divisions. This means that values of interest can be calculated for specific divisions or company segments.

This update enables precise value calculations, offering a deeper understanding of business impact. By filtering irrelevant data, assessments become more accurate and meaningful, helping you prioritize improvements effectively. Furthermore, values were calculated to get more precise by excluding data that improvement activities couldn't address.

SAP Signavio Process Insights – Value calculation for different divisions/company segments

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature.

Get insights into your active usage rates

Additionally, we've introduced active usage rates for process performance indicators related to master data and customizations. This new metric helps quickly identify active object usage, providing a clear picture of system utilization and pinpointing areas for improvement. Users will now be able to understand their performance and improvement potential quickly. Furthermore, the actively used percentage will also be able to be used for historical trending and industry benchmarking as a future feature.

SAP Signavio Process Insights – Insights into active usage rates

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature.

Visualize system configuration in the administration screen

A new administration tab enhances transparency into connected systems, allowing access to detailed system information, including connection types and installed plug-in versions, allowing for better adjustment to requirements. With this deeper insights, users can manage systems more effectively and optimize performance.

SAP Signavio Process Insights – System Configuration Visualization in the administration screen

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature.

Get external benchmarks for process flow detailed metrics

Lastly, external benchmarking now includes detailed process flow metrics, offering insights across 2,200+ metrics to evaluate process performance, identify and prioritize improvement areas, and set realistic targets based on external benchmarks.

SAP Signavio Process Insights – external benchmarks for process flow detailed metrics

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature.

Process and Journey Modeling

As highlighted earlier, the new text-to-process feature is a standout addition to SAP Signavio Process Manager. However, it is just one of several enhancements driving improvements in this release.

SAP Signavio Process Manager 

Improved user notification for integration of SAP Signavio to SAP Cloud ALM

Gain greater control and visibility with detailed user notifications and real-time feedback on synchronization success or failure between SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM. This feature ensures immediate insights, allowing process owners to monitor and manage synchronizations effectively.

SAP Signavio Process Manager - Improved user notification for integration of SAP Signavio to SAP Cloud ALM

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature.

Seamless Activity Deletion Sync: Keeping SAP Signavio Solution Manager Up to Date

Align data across systems by synchronizing deletion flags from SAP Signavio Process Manager to SAP Solution Manager, ensuring system consistency and minimizing manual errors. This automated process reduces risks by accurately reflecting deleted activities, enhancing data reliability.

SAP Signavio Process Manager – Activity Deletion Sync

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature.

Greater Control with Direct Synchronization Management

Give users greater control by allowing them to cancel active synchronizations directly from the connector interface, eliminating the need for SAP support. This feature reduces wait times, minimizes downtime, and enhances efficiency for a smoother user experience.

SAP Signavio Process Manager – Direct Synchronization Management

Click here to start a step-by/step tutorial for this feature.

Exporting executable assignments to tasks and activities to SAP Solution Manager

Maintaining data accuracy across systems is crucial for seamless operations. With SAP Signavio Process Manager as the leading system for executable assignments, you now can minimize manual effort while ensuring all updates and task assignments are automatically reflected in SAP Solution Manager.

This enhancement eliminates the need for manual updates, reducing errors and maintaining data consistency by automatically removing executable assignments in SAP Solution Manager when they are deleted in SAP Signavio. By streamlining this process, you can increase efficiency, reduce administrative workload, and maintain system alignment effortlessly.

SAP Signavio Process Manager – Exporting Assignments to SAP Solution Manager

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature. 

Improved error handling for synchronization with SAP Solution Manager

Enhance transparency and efficiency in synchronization management by enabling users to download sync logs for offline review. This feature allows for detailed analysis of synchronization details and error resolution, ensuring smoother troubleshooting and process optimization.

With clear visibility into data flows, users can better understand synchronization processes and track sync direction in the history log, making data management more intuitive and accessible.

SAP Signavio Process Manager –Error handling for synchronization with SAP Solution Manager

Support additional data types when synchronizing process models

Ensure a more comprehensive process representation by enabling the synchronization of fixed-value attributes, such as drop-downs and numbers, between SAP Signavio and SAP Solution Manager. This enhancement improves data accuracy and completeness, ensuring that key attributes are consistently mapped and transferred for better process alignment and reliability.
SAP Signavio Process Manager – Additional data type support for process models synchronization

Enhanced SAP Enable Now integration capabilities

Last, but not least, the enhanced integration between SAP Enable Now and SAP Signavio solutions streamlines documentation creation. By supporting a broader range of SAP Signavio object types and enabling role-specific documentation, this integration increases flexibility and improves usability for authors.

SAP Signavio Process Manager – Enable Now integration Capabilities

SAP Signavio Journey Modeler and SAP Signavio Process Governance

Streamlining journey approvals with automation in SAP Signavio Journey Modeler and SAP Signavio Process Governance

Keeping journey models aligned with your best practices and regulations is essential for consistency and compliance. With the automated approvals feature in SAP Signavio Journey Modeler, journey modelers can effortlessly submit revisions to designated journey owners and stakeholders, ensuring a structured and efficient approval process.

By eliminating time-consuming manual workflows, this feature accelerates publication cycles, reduces costs, and frees up valuable resources. With a more streamlined approval mechanism, you can maintain consistency, enhance compliance, and improve operational efficiency—all while focusing on delivering better journey experiences.

The introduction of approvals for journey models in SAP Signavio Process Governance brings customizable workflows that streamline the journey approval process. This feature ensures efficient validation of modelled journeys by allowing journey modelers to request approvals for new models or changes while maintaining a robust audit trail. Real-time updates on model approval status changes save valuable time and enhance process efficiency.

SAP Signavio Journey Modeler and SAP Signavio Process Governance – Streamlining Journey Approvals

Process Governance and Automated Execution

SAP Signavio Process Governance 

Enable secure credential handling for activity interface

The new credential handling for activity interface feature in SAP Signavio Process Governance enables the secure and efficient integration of third-party systems into workflows. By providing safe storage for third-party user credentials, it ensures data protection and compliance while simplifying workflow integration.

By using a credential management system, you can seamlessly incorporate external systems, reducing manual effort and enhancing overall efficiency.

SAP Signavio Process Governance – Credential handling for activity interface

Simplify and streamline document upload with Microsoft SharePoint workflow activity

The new Microsoft SharePoint integration workflow activity for file upload simplifies document upload within the governance workflow. This integration feature within the activity interface ensures secure management and maintenance of your documents.

With automated document uploads, you can ensure accurate placement of files in the correct Microsoft SharePoint location, reducing the risk of human error and enhancing visibility and control for better workflow management. It enables easier, more accessible file uploads directly within workflows, streamlines processes for increased efficiency while safeguarding compliance and data integrity.

Improve document upload with smooth Microsoft SharePoint integration workflow activity with automated, template-based activity for streamlined governance.

SAP Signavio Process Governance – Microsoft SharePoint integration workflow activity – upload file

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature. 

Gain more flexibility in managing case views

Choose the best view for your needs with the new alternative case overview screen, designed to empower admins to select the most suitable display for their workspace. This feature allows you to tailor case views to their unique requirements, ensuring the display best supports their workflow and operational needs.

With flexible viewing options, admins can simplify case management, making it easier to navigate and analyze cases efficiently. This enhancement is particularly beneficial for large workspaces, as it helps reduce page load times, improving usability and performance.

By providing greater control over how cases are displayed, this update enhances efficiency, usability, and overall workflow optimization—ensuring a smoother case management experience.

SAP Signavio Process Governance – Flexibility in managing case views

 SAP Signavio Process Governance – Flexibility in managing case views

Streamline the user off-boarding process

The new user off-boarding feature streamlines the off-boarding process by eliminating unnecessary replacement assignments when no workflows are assigned to users. This enhancement improves performance, reduces administrative effort, and enhances efficiency in managing inactive users.

SAP Signavio Process Governance – Enhancement of user off-boarding

 

Process transformation management and collaboration 

SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub

Streamline collaboration for more effective decision-making using SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub

The new enhanced Fact Sheet in SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub provides a comprehensive view of key process information enriched with additional metadata. This feature integrates detailed process data into the existing view and includes export functionality, allowing users to download fact sheets as PDFs for offline access and easy sharing. By ensuring consistently accessible and shareable fact sheets, this enhancement streamlines collaboration and improves understanding for more effective decision-making.

SAP Process Collaboration Hub – Streamlining collaboration for better decision making

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature. 

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Management

Quantify the value of transformation initiatives with SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager

In SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager, the value analysis capability empowers you to quantify the monetary value of transformation initiatives. It enables you to prioritize and present initiatives to stakeholders based on measurable ROI ensuring data-driven decision-making rather than relying on intuition.

You can uncover the monetary value of identified inefficiencies across initiatives, compare their impact, and adopt a value-driven approach to prioritize efforts—ensuring maximum returns on your transformation efforts.

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager - Value Analysis: Identify the monetary impact of transformation initiatives

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature.

Capture insights from benchmarking analytics

Easily capture insights from benchmarking analytics in SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager and incorporate them into transformation initiatives for a centralized view of key findings. By leveraging these insights, you can ensure consistency and continuity in their process improvements, driving more informed and strategic decision-making.

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager – Create centralized insights for metrics in benchmarking analytics

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature. 

Value Accelerators

Enhanced Value Accelerator Library

Restrict visibility of Value Accelerator Library to authorized users 

Empower admins to control access to the SAP Signavio Value Accelerator Library, ensuring it is available only to authorized users. This feature allows you to design the use of Value Accelerators based on internal processes and compliance requirements, preventing unnecessary communication with users who do not require access.

By restricting visibility, you can streamline internal processes, minimize communication overhead, and ensure that Value Accelerators are used efficiently by your teams.

SAP Signavio Value Accelerator Library – Visibility Restriction

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature. 

SAP Signavio Process Manager

Value accelerator for process design and standardization for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 

SAP Signavio Process Manager now includes the latest SAP best-practice business process models to facilitate seamless implementation and accelerate transitions to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. These models streamline "fit-to-standard" assessments and enable you to easily explore and adopt best-practice processes. Industry-specific best-practice reference content, such as that tailored to the agribusiness sector, helps users design optimized process landscapes, significantly reducing time to value.

SAP Signavio Process Manager - Process design and standardization for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Click here to start a step-by-step tutorial for this feature. 

Value accelerator for process design for Agricultural Contract Management with SAP solutions 

Users can now speed up time to value by exploring, utilizing, and building on industry-specific best-practice reference process content in SAP Signavio Process Manager, tailored to the agribusiness sector. This enables the design of optimized process landscapes, making procedures more efficient and reducing complexity by providing a common language for users.

By leveraging SAP reference architecture content, you can better understand end-to-end processes and create structured process architectures aligned with industry standards, ensuring a streamlined and effective approach to process management.

Improvement to Value Accelerator for Order to Cash in SAP ERP Central Component and SAP S/4HANA

With harmonized Value Accelerators, you can drive transformation projects more effectively while increasing coverage, event accuracy, and transparency across end-to-end Order-to-Cash processes. Additionally, enhanced flexibility allows businesses to adapt systems and process customizations with greater ease, ensuring a more agile and responsive approach to process management.

SAP Signavio Process Insights & SAP Signavio Process Intelligence: Order to Cash, SAP ERP Central Component

Plug and gain approach

With historical versions of SQL transformation scripts, data modelers can confidently change data views, event collectors, and case attributes collectors. Previous versions can be easily restored in case the changes should be reverted, ensuring minimal disruption. Additionally, data modelers benefit from the ability to compare versions to the current working draft and quickly highlight differences, enabling faster debugging and helping ensure accurate data transformations.

 

The SAP Signavio February 2025 Release brings powerful enhancements to the SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite, helping you accelerate process optimization, gain AI-driven insights, and seamlessly integrate systems. With new AI capabilities, enhanced usability, and improved governance features, this release empowers you to drive measurable transformation outcomes with greater efficiency and precision.

Stay ahead in your transformation journey—explore these features today and unlock new levels of business performance.

 

We are looking forward to seeing you on the SAP Signavio Community to share feedback, questions and discuss with your peers! This is your space!

 

Please feel free to share your questions around the release or feedback in the comments with us. You can also reach out to us via the SAP Signavio Community or alternatively watch our Release Webcast on-demand.

entry.title: Re: Mobile Start-Push Notification
entry.content:
Dear @BilalFaikoglu,

the configuration process to expose notifications from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and thus also SAP Mobile Start is explained in detail in the Learning Journey "Setting up SAP Mobile Start with SAP S/4HANA on SAP Build Work Zone". In there, it exists a dedicated unit for "Receiving SAP S/4HANA Workflow Notifications".

Logically the first step would be to successfully expose the notification to be visible in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. Push notifications to SAP Mobile Start do work automatically if everything is configured correctly. A typical pitfall for push notifications to fail to be delivered is a mismatch of the user identifier.

If you are unable to solve the configuration with the mentioned learning journey, you may reach out to our product management team by writing to our central inbox: sapmobilestart@sap.com

Kind regards,
Thomas

entry.title: Error message : Navigation is not supported for role XXX .
entry.content:
How can I view the details for a particular Role that is assigned to a Business Partner via Manage Business Partner Master Data App.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Error message : Navigation is not supported for role...
entry.content:
App Maintain Business Partner (APP ID: BP) can be used to maintain/navigate data for roles other than Customer or Supplier.

Steps:

  1. Go to Maintain Business partner app
  2. Search for the Business partner
  3. Navigate to Display in BP role tab
  4. Select the respective role

entry.title: Create By Association for 1:1 composition in RAP Unmanaged
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a requirement where I have a composition (parent-child relationship) of cardinality 1:1.
When I click on Edit and create a parent, I want the child section to be editable as well. So that I can enter values for both parent and child and create a record.

But that is not how the system is behaving.
Is there any special way of handling such 1:1 compositions?

In the below screenshot, LimitingRule is the child and should also be editable when creating a Trigger entity.
The parent(Trigger Details) is editable but not the child(LimitingRule).

Thanks in advance!!
Shivam Chadha

entry.title: Inbound delivery in S4HANA goods receipt without EWM
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a question, we are using Storage locations to store materials. No warehouse management.

Our scenario is supplier sends the material to a port where a GR is done but actuall material is stored in the warehouse storage location.

Supplier  > Port  (GR) > Storage location (WH)

Can we do a GR is the Port and do a internal posting from SL to SL automatically once quality is approved?

entry.title: How to add integration flow version to response header?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

Is there a way to get below version number from a header or property value anywhere inside the flow?

SAP Integration Suite SAP Build

entry.title: Re: We are unable to book CER006 EXAM, not showing any booking information or just grade out, Pelase
entry.content: It has been answered tens of times these last weeks. See here: https://www.google.com/search?q=cer006%20site:sap.com

entry.title: C4C report: working days current month
entry.content:
Hello experts,

i have a requirement to set up a report which includes closed tickets per working day for the actual month.

I have not found an option yet to just include working days (monday - friday).

Any ideas how to set up?

Thank you and best regards,

Max

entry.title: building Dynamic XML file
entry.content:
Hello experts ,

I'm searching for a way to build dynamic XML file 

we are building xml file for analytics team . the general format is not complex or nested the only thing is the number of column is variable . 

the format of the populated xml table .

[employee 1 ] , [ var value 1 ] , [ var value 2] , [ var value 3] .

[employee 2 ] , [ var value 1 ] , [ var value 2] , [ var value 3] ,[ var value 4] ,[ var value 5] ,

....etc 

I'm thinking of handling it as string concatenating the tags and building it myself .

is there any better way to do it ? I looked into cl_xml_document but couldn't find good example to implement something similar to my requirement .

Please help .

entry.title: Re: When there is a data change in Product master, can we...
entry.content:
Dear @Rajeevpillai , 

I would like to confirm that, currently, it is not feasible to implement an alert for changes in the material master due to the unavailability of a BAdI for such changes. Given the importance of this functionality from a business perspective, I highly recommend that you raise this requirement via the SAP Influence Portal. This will allow the Product Team to review and potentially incorporate it into their SAP roadmap.

You can find the relevant details for submitting your request on the following link:
Submitting New Feature Requests via SAP Customer Influence Portal.


Best Regards , 
Harvind

entry.title: Re: Removing leading zeroes using XPATH in Content Modifier
entry.content: Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: Does SAP Mobile Start support Zebra laser barcode sca...
entry.content: @BrendanFarthing SAP Mobile Start supports laser scan from SAP UI5 apps. You need to press the physical scanning button on the Zebra device to trigger the laser scan.

entry.title: Re: When there is a data change in Product master, can we...
entry.content: Thanks OwenLiu and Harvind for the comments. Customer Influence is already created for this request.

entry.title: Initial Setup – Preparing for SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Signavio Integration
entry.content:
In this first article of our series, we explore the initial steps to integrate SAP Cloud ALM with SAP Signavio. Learn how to request an SAP Signavio Connectivity Add-On and begin the integration with SAP Cloud ALM.

 

  1. The first step is to go to https://me.sap.com/systemsprovisioning. In the 'Provisioning' tab, click the 'Start provisioning' button to request the SAP Signavio connectivity add-on.
  2. On this screen, you will need to select three pieces of information. The first is the user authentication, where you need to choose the correct SAP Cloud Identity services.
  3. Next, you need to select the SAP Signavio system you want to integrate with, followed by the SAP Cloud ALM system.
  4. After clicking 'Submit',you will see the confirmation of the request. 
  5. all that's left to do is wait.

After receiving the confirmation of the connectivity add-one creation, you can follow the next blog to proceed with the synchronization process in the follwoing URL; 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/synchronization-in-action-executing-the-integration-between-sap-cloud-alm/ba-p/14005369

entry.title: Synchronization in Action – Executing the Integration Between SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Signavio
entry.content:
In the previous blog (Initial Setup – Preparing for SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Signavio Integration), we explained the steps to start the provisioning process for a SAP Signavio connectivity Add-on. In this blog, we’ll cover how to synchronize SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM.

  1. The first step is to open the process you want to synchronize in the Process Collaboration Hub (This is crucial because if you don't do this, the synchronization option won't appear).
  2. To perform the synchronization, we need to open the process in 'Preview' mode. To enter 'Preview' mode, click on your user icon and select the 'View' option, then choose 'Preview'. This will change the screen (you’ll see the preview white/blue line ).
  3. Now, in the options menu (the three dots in the toolbar), you need to click in the option-->'Sync with SAP Cloud ALM'.
  4. A new screen will open that you need to confirm. This screen explains the revision schema and some restrictions.
  5. After the sync is complete, a confirmation button will appear to confirm the success of the activity. You can also click on the 'Open in SAP Cloud ALM' option to jump directly to the corresponding process.
  6. Now you can see the process in SAP Cloud ALM in 'Draft' status. Notice that the process has a lock icon, meaning that it needs to be maintained in the Signavio tool. If you are an administrator in SAP Cloud ALM, you will be able to unlock the process to modify it in CALM.

To recap, this synchronization process between SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM is just one small part of the complete lifecycle that needs to be defined.

Other points to consider:

  • Defining a proper lifecycle for process maintenance is crucial, understanding in which tool and who is responsible for the activities is a key definition.
  • Defining the correct integration with LeanIX to achieve greater synergy and reduce duplicated efforts.
  • The integration between SAP Cloud ALM and EnableNow is another key element to ensure a clear path for system transformation

entry.title: No Successor App available for Manage Catalog Item Recommendations (F3293)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,

Good day!

Raising this request for assistance to look/find a possible successor or related app to Manage Catalog Item Recommendations that can still do the same functionality.

We appreciate any help/assistance in any way.

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: No Successor App available for Manage Catalog Item Re...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

This kind of query should be raised via the SAP Communities. 

In relation to the App, when I check in the Fiori Apps Library: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F3149')/S29OP
There is no mention of the App being depreciated, it is still available and valid. 

If you have further queries on an app's lifespan, functionalities or configuration please always check in the Fiori Apps Library. 

I hope this helps, 
Kind Regards, 
Lisa 
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: RAP : Trying to make a child with composition relation [0..1] but in object page no edit mode t
entry.content: Have you found any solution for this?

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA (Bangalore, India)
entry.content: Great, looking forward to this session!

entry.title: Re: RAP - create child with IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE
entry.content: Have you found any solution for this? I have also raised the same problem : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/create-by-association-for-1-1-composition-in-rap-unmanaged/qaq-p/14006338

entry.title: Re: Access for users to update allocation rules in SAC
entry.content:
Hi @Vivek27 

regarding your first question

  1. What access will be needed for users to update allocation rules? Is there any other way without giving developer role?

you need planning professional role. Since Allocation steps migrated to data actions a planning standard user cannot maintain the allocation steps. It is a bit contradictive because in the legacy allocation designer a planning standard user was able to maintain those steps.

br,

thomas

entry.title: Employee is not receiving notifications from MLL, Her supervisor is receiving them overdue.
entry.content:
One employee is not receiving her MLL course overdue notifications. It appears there is an issue with her MLL profile. While other employees are receiving their notifications, she is not receiving hers. However, she does receive notifications for her direct reports. Brittney has confirmed that she used to receive these notifications before, but currently her manager is receiving her overdue email notifications.

I have checked her Outlook settings to ensure there are no rules or logic affecting this, and we have successfully sent notifications to her manually.

Could you please assist in identifying any profile changes that might prevent an employee from receiving their own notifications?
Employee ID: Brittney Blizard ‎(U773214)‎

entry.title: Re: Safety Data Sheet Customization and Generation
entry.content: Hi @harrystrauss  I believe you can use the Apply Regulations - Safety Data Sheets to add a language for an SDS

Regards,

Marcel

entry.content: This solution fixed the problem for me, thank you.

entry.title: Program item is not recording when it's completed outside the program
entry.content:
Program item is not recording when its completed outside

 

We have a program with 4 items and one of the item in that 4 item, user has completed outside which is like normal item completion.

Will that outside completed item completion come over to the program status as well?

If not what are the possibilities

entry.title: Re: How to restrict authorization for contract activation...
entry.content:
Hi Dianna,

Please update the specific business role or create a copy of business role "BR_RECM_SPECIALIST" and update restrictions using tile "Maintain business role".

Best regards,

Achyuth reddy K.

entry.title: Re: Rental Process in SAP Field Logistics
entry.content: Hi @Aleem : You can initiate the returns of rental items using "Initiate returns by Products" App. It should create a STO/OBD to move the goods back to onshore supply base.

entry.title: How to Analyze "CAMEL_CONTEXT_NOT_STARTED" Issue in CPI
entry.content:
Overview:
Based on our previous support for our valued customers and partners in developing integration adapters for cloud platform integration, we’ve identified that some of our partners/customers are encountering issues while qualifying their adapters. This blog will guide you on how to resolve the "CAMEL_CONTEXT_NOT_STARTED" issue in integration flows.

 

How to Analyze "CAMEL_CONTEXT_NOT_STARTED" Issue:
This issue typically arises when a class is not properly loaded. Class loading can fail due to "Dependency issues" or "An error in the adapter code, which throws an exception during the object creation at the class level".

Step 1: Identify Which Adapter Variant is Affected
First, we need to determine whether the issue lies with the sender adapter, receiver adapter, or both. We can isolate this by creating and deploying basic integration flows with the following configurations:

Sender Adapter: Create and deploy a basic integration flow with only the sender adapter.
Receiver Adapter: Create and deploy a basic integration flow with only the receiver adapter.
Both Adapters: Create and deploy a basic integration flow with both sender and receiver adapters.

Findings from Step 1:
Based on the results, we can narrow down the issue to the specific code in our adapter:
If the issue is on the sender side: Further investigate the consumer component of the integration adapter.
If the issue is on the receiver side: Further investigate the producer component of the integration adapter.
If the issue is on both sides: The issue is likely within the endpoint class.


Step 2: Add Additional Logs to Adapter Code
To understand the root cause, we need to add more logs to the adapter code.

For sender adapter issues:
Move all consumer class-level object initializations to the constructor.
Add logs after every initialization to trace the execution flow.

For receiver adapter issues:
Move all producer class-level object initializations to the constructor.
Add logs after every initialization to trace the execution flow.

For issues in both variants:
Move all endpoint class-level object initializations to the constructor.
Add logs after every initialization to trace the execution flow.
By adding these logs, you can pinpoint where the failure is occurring and resolve the issue effectively.

 

Thanks
